event: Archangel Michael Defeating Satan and Guardian Angel
symbols: Archangel Michael with raised sword trampling Satan — the triumph of good over evil and divine victory over the forces of darkness
Guardian angel in white robes with censer — intercession and the heavenly ministry of protection for the faithful
God the Father in the upper lunette panels — divine authority presiding over the angelic hosts
arched frame with gilded ornament — the heavenly vault and the sacredness of devotional objects
fallen demon underfoot — the subjugation of sin and rebellion against God
description: A small devotional triptych set in an ornate arched wooden frame with gilded foliate scrollwork along the edges. The two main panels depict full-length angels against blue skies with rocky landscapes. On the left panel, a warrior angel in red and blue armor raises a sword overhead while trampling a dark, defeated figure beneath his feet. On the right panel, an angel in flowing white robes stands with one arm raised and the other lowered, holding what appears to be a censer.
theological_description: This devotional triptych presents the two great angelic ministries celebrated in Scripture: the warrior archangel Michael who battles the forces of darkness, and the guardian angel who ministers to the saints. The left panel depicts Michael in the act described in Revelation 12:7-9, where war breaks out in heaven and Michael and his angels fight against the dragon, casting Satan down to earth. Michael is shown in classical military attire with sword raised, his foot planted on the defeated adversary, echoing the promise of Genesis 3:15 that the serpent's head would be crushed. The right panel shows an angel of gentler ministry — the guardian or ministering spirit referenced in Hebrews 1:14, 'Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?' The censer evokes the angel of Revelation 8:3-4 who offers incense with the prayers of the saints before the golden altar. Above both panels, the presence of God the Father in the lunettes establishes divine sovereignty over all angelic activity. Together the triptych affirms the dual reality of the unseen spiritual world: fierce combat against principalities and powers (Ephesians 6:12) and tender guardianship of the faithful (Psalm 91:11-12).

scripture_refs.reason: Michael and his angels fighting the dragon is the direct scene depicted in the left panel
The archangel Michael contending with the devil is a foundational text for the iconography of Michael's spiritual combat
The right panel's ministering angel embodies the concept of angels as ministering spirits sent to serve the heirs of salvation
The guardian angel figure reflects the promise that God commands angels to guard the faithful in all their ways
The censer held by the angel on the right evokes the angel offering incense with the prayers of the saints
The overall triptych reflects the spiritual warfare against principalities and powers in heavenly places
